1.notes: Database of 60 yarns in scoring.js (YARNS), each with brand, name, weight class, fiber %s, yards, grams, typical gauge, machine-wash flag, price tier 1-3, and surface texture. Verified against manufacturer ball-band sources June 2026 (task 30, 54 records, 9 corrections), then expanded 54 -> 60 via the tracker-19 thin-cell pass (+6 ball-band-verified yarns: Bulky/Lace/Aran depth + 2 tweeds). Changelog in docs/spec-verification-2026-06.md.

2.notes: WEIGHTS (8 classes), FIBERS (12), FAMILY (animal/plant/synthetic) and TEXTURES (6) defined in scoring.js.

3.notes: score() in scoring.js: same weight class = 32 pts, adjacent = 16, returns null (excluded) when >1 class apart.

4.notes: Yards-per-gram thickness term, up to 22 pts, scaled by a symmetric relative difference (measured against the mean of the two values).

5.notes: Gauge term: up to 13 pts (3 pts lost per stitch of difference); 7 pts neutral credit when target/candidate gauge is unknown.

6.notes: fiberSimilarity(): 60% exact-fiber overlap + 40% family overlap, scaled to 18 pts; wool and merino treated as near-equivalent. Covered by tests.

7.notes: Care term: 5 pts unless the target is machine-washable and the candidate is not (only that loss is penalized). The intentional asymmetry is documented by a test.

8.notes: whyText() builds the per-card rationale (weight, thickness %, fiber character, texture note, machine-wash warning) from the same numbers score() uses.

9.notes: Pick mode: yarnSelect dropdown populated and alpha-sorted in init(); getTarget() reads the selected yarn.

10.notes: Specs mode form: weight, yards/ball, grams/ball, optional gauge, main fiber + %, optional secondary fiber, optional texture, machine-wash checkbox; parsed and validated in app.js.

11.notes: Care, price-tier, and fiber-family filters applied in findSubs().

12.notes: Pick/specs toggle is now a proper ARIA tablist (role=tab/tabpanel, aria-selected, arrow-key navigation) in app.js setTab().

13.notes: Full responsive redesign ('editorial atelier'): fluid type, grids collapse at 720px and 440px, cards reflow, buy links go full-width on small screens.

14.notes: findSubs() scores, filters, sorts desc, keeps score >= 55, renders the top 10 as cards with a score ring, swatch, spec pills, rationale, and buy links.

15.notes: buyLinks() generates retailer search URLs from a RETAILERS config (LoveCrafts, Hobbii, Amazon) with an affiliate-param hook. Real tags tracked under task 26.

16.notes: Empty-state and below-quality-bar messaging rendered when no candidates pass; styled error panel for invalid specs. June 2026: added a 'stretch match' reveal — when fewer than 3 candidates clear the 55-point bar, the best 1-2 just-below-bar candidates (>=40) render as de-emphasized, clearly-labelled stretch cards (dashed border, muted ring) instead of only a hidden count; turns thin/empty results into actionable options.

17.notes: README rewritten — overview, run/test instructions, scoring breakdown, project layout, data note, deploy, roadmap, disclaimer.

18.notes: Runs end-to-end as a build-free static project (index.html + styles.css + scoring.js + app.js). Smoke-tested in headless Edge (initial load + results). Cross-browser tracked under task 28.

19.notes: DB expanded to 60. Earlier June 2026 gap-fill (linen, bamboo, chainette, chenille, Aran/cashmere depth) closed the selectable-but-empty fibers (guarded by a test); chainette is represented by Knit Picks Lindy Chain after Comfy Worsted was corrected to smooth (task 30). The tracker-19 thin-cell pass then added 6 ball-band-verified yarns for the sparsest cells: Bulky depth (Cascade 128 Superwash, Malabrigo Chunky), Lace (Drops Lace), Aran (Stylecraft Special Aran), and a 2nd/3rd tweed (Tahki Donegal Tweed, Drops Soft Tweed) so the DK Felted Tweed now has a same-weight tweed twin. New relationships locked by substitution fixtures; before/after match-depth verified via a Python port of score(). Residual: chainette stays a single fingering entry (a fine-weight pair is rare), and synthetic-Bulky / halo stay thin partly by soft-gate design.

20.notes: Texture/halo descriptor added to every record (smooth/tweed/chainette/roving/halo/chenille) via the TEXTURES field.

21.notes: Texture term added to score() with a pairwise similarity matrix; point weights rebalanced (32/22/18/13/10/5) so totals still cap at 100. Covered by tests.

22.notes: Validated against yarnsub.com (expert substitution authority) for 8 flagship yarns June 2026: engine matched clean fiber archetypes well but under-weighted fiber family + texture for fiber/texture-defined yarns. Fix applied and signed off: a multiplicative soft-gate in score() (FAMILY_FLOOR 0.65, TEXTURE_FLOOR 0.60) demotes cross-family / distinctive-texture mismatches; substitution fixtures expanded to 7 cases. Writeup: docs/scoring-validation-2026-06.md. Residuals deferred: fiber blend-ratio nuance (minor) and DB coverage gaps (task 19).

23.notes: Resolved: getTarget() reads an explicit optional secondary-fiber dropdown; the <100% remainder is assigned to it, or left unaccounted when unknown — no acrylic-or-wool guess.

24.notes: Resolved: validateSpecs() validates yards/grams (>0, sane caps), optional gauge (4-50 or blank), and main fiber % (1-100); offending fields get an .invalid outline and an inline error list.

25.notes: Accessibility pass: ARIA tablist + keyboard nav, semantic landmarks, associated labels, :focus-visible rings, prefers-reduced-motion, score conveyed by number+label (not colour alone). All 12 text/background pairs verified WCAG AA.

26.notes: Region-aware buy links shipped (PR #6, 2026-06-27): buyLinks(y, region) reads a region-keyed RETAILERS config, region saved in the URL. 2026-06-27 follow-up: every retailer now declares an affiliate `aff.network`, and buyLinks() routes each URL through affiliateUrl() — a per-network wrapper (Amazon Associates ?tag= and Awin cread.php are verified formats; CJ automated-deep-link is scaffolded, pending CJ-dashboard confirmation; ShareASale/Impact get a case on adoption). Publisher IDs live in AFFILIATE_IDS and start EMPTY, so links stay plain (zero tracking) until tags are dropped in. Added Michaels Canada to RETAILERS.CA (now Yarnspirations/Mary Maxim/Michaels Canada/Amazon.ca on CJ/Awin/CJ/Amazon; US LoveCrafts/Hobbii/Amazon on Awin/CJ/Amazon). Candidate retailers, networks, and dropped options recorded in docs/affiliate-candidates.md. Suite green (node --test, 42). Still in-progress: obtain real publisher IDs (CJ PID, Awin affid, Amazon Associates tags per marketplace) + confirm the CJ link format before going live; optional cross-list of LoveCrafts/Hobbii into CA and add Knit Picks/WeCrochet (ShareASale). 2026-07-02 monetization prep: affiliate-disclosure line (with the required Amazon Associates wording) added to the site footer + README, and LICENSE added (source-available, all rights reserved — deliberately NOT open source so the deployed site + affiliate links can't be legally re-hosted with swapped tags; repo stays public for free Pages).

27.notes: Target yarn + filters encoded into the URL hash; restored and auto-run on load; Copy-link button. Results also re-run live (debounced) after the first search.

28.notes: Verified in headless Edge/Chromium and Firefox 152 (Gecko), June 2026 — full result render incl. score-ring + swatch SVG, spec pills, buy-links, and the new yarn records; reduced-motion path also confirmed. Only Safari (WebKit) remains, and it is Mac-only so it stays with the user.

29.notes: Automated tests cover scoring edge cases. Manual UI extremes verified June 2026 via headless-Edge renders: Lace + Super Bulky (both weight boundaries), full 10-card specs result, displayScore 99-cap, empty/below-bar state, and partial-fiber custom specs (unknown secondary/gauge/texture). Reduced-motion render path also confirmed. Interactive cross-browser rolls into task 28/34.

30.notes: Full per-record accuracy pass completed June 2026: all 54 records checked against manufacturer ball-band pages + yarnsub.com/Ravelry via a per-manufacturer research fan-out. 9 corrections applied (gauge x6, yardage, Drops Andes -> Super Bulky, Felted Tweed fiber -> viscose, Comfy texture -> smooth); added viscose fiber + Knit Picks Lindy Chain (chainette). Changelog, judgment-call rationale, and residual notes in docs/spec-verification-2026-06.md. Residual: Knit Picks Shadow gauge unverified (discontinued, no findable ball-band number); optional physical ball-band re-confirmation worksheet at docs/spec-review-checklist.md.

31.notes: Deferred for v1. Live stock/price checking needs a backend or third-party API; the app is intentionally static.

32.notes: GitHub Pages deploy workflow added (.github/workflows/pages.yml). Root cause of the long-failing deploy: Pages needs a public repo on the free plan (private requires a paid plan), not a token/config issue. Resolved 2026-06-27: repo made public after a clean full-history secret scan, Pages enabled via API (build_type=workflow), PR #5 added configure-pages enablement:true (now functional). Deploy green in 15s; site verified LIVE (HTTP 200) at https://shepherd70.github.io/yarn-swap/.

33.notes: README updated to reflect the current feature set (texture scoring, shareable URLs, swatches, accessibility, tests, deploy).

34.notes: Production build now deployed live at https://shepherd70.github.io/yarn-swap/ and smoke-verified 2026-06-27 (HTTP 200, correct title/markup served). Data sign-off (30) done. Full sign-off still pending the Safari/WebKit pass (28, Mac-only, user) and a visual pass over the live build.

35.notes: Direct product-page links shipped 2026-07-02: sparse PRODUCTS map in scoring.js (key `b|n` × retailer name), buyLinks() prefers a curated product URL over the search fallback (data-buy=direct/search, tinted+arrow style for direct), composing with the task-26 affiliate wrapper. Shape enforced by tests/products.test.js (suite 51 green); working sheet generated by scripts/gen-product-checklist.js -> docs/product-link-checklist.md. First curation pass (Amazon, 15 mass-market yarns x .ca/.com, live-verified) seeded 4 clean .ca listings — key finding: Amazon sells yarn lines almost entirely as per-COLORWAY child ASINs (amazon.com had zero clean parents), so most Amazon cells correctly stay on search; see docs/product-link-verification-2026-06.md. Next: curate the yarn-store retailers (Yarnspirations/Mary Maxim/LoveCrafts/Hobbii — one canonical page per line, no colorway problem) and re-check the fetch-blocked ⚠️ Amazon candidates in a real browser.
